description: Authoritative inventory of files in the Claude-research/ workspace (the active project subdirectory of the task-research/ repository). Hand-edited; file_inventory.md is regenerated from this file by code/data_management/regenerate_derived_files.py.
schema: Each section has section_id, title, intro, and files[]. Each file entry has path (workspace-relative), description, status, and optionally generated_by, generated_from, type.

sections.title: Primary Data Files — Authoritative
sections.intro: Hand-edited or carefully promoted; treat as source of truth. Do not overwrite without diff verification.

files.description: 172 cognitive processes across 19 categories. Each process has process_id (hed_<slug>), process_name, category_id, definition, and optional aliases, references, tasks, task_count, notes. The tasks[] field is derived from task_details.json by code/data_management/regenerate_derived_files.py and should not be hand-edited. Schema validated by schemas/process_details.schema.json.

103 experimental tasks/paradigms. Top-level is a bare array (not a wrapping object). Each task has hedtsk_id (hedtsk_<slug>), canonical_name, aliases (string list), short_definition, description, inclusion_test, variations, atlas_id, source_task_numbers, status, hed_process_ids, references. Schema validated by schemas/task_details.schema.json.
This file. Authoritative inventory of the workspace. Hand-edit to add/update entries; file_inventory.md is regenerated from it.
Flat alphabetical projection of task_details.json. Each entry: hedtsk_id, canonical_name, short_definition, aliases, variation_names.
Reverse index from process_id to its associated tasks. Used to populate the tasks[] field on each process in process_details.json.
Per-task list of process links (task-centric view).
Human-readable rendering of file_inventory.json as markdown tables.

sections.title: Project Conventions
sections.intro: Read by every Claude session at start. CLAUDE.md and the top-level README live at the repository root (one level above Claude-research/), not inside the catalog workspace.

files.description: Project conventions: repository layout, file placement rules, reference schema, path conventions, sandbox file-access quirks, session reporting, API keys, core-data-files handling rules, .scratch/ usage, literature-search quick reference.
Top-level project README: setup (venv, dependencies), VS Code wiring, how to run validators and the literature-search phases, troubleshooting.

sections.title: Criteria and Reference Documents
sections.intro: Definitions of what counts as a process, what counts as a task, naming conventions, borderline policies.

files.description: Process classification criteria: what counts as a process, single-answer inclusion test, scope boundaries, naming conventions.
Task classification criteria: what counts as a task, what counts as a variation, the 13 DROP categories, borderline policy questions, naming conventions.

sections.title: JSON Schemas (draft-07)
sections.intro: Declarative shape definitions for the two catalog files. Wired into VS Code via .vscode/settings.json for inline editor validation; consumed by validators in code/data_management/.

files.description: Schema for process_details.json: required keys, types, id patterns (hed_<slug>), reference roles enum, alias and variation shape with additionalProperties:false.
Schema for task_details.json: top-level array, required task fields, hedtsk_<slug> and hed_<slug> id patterns, three-key inclusion_test shape, variation shape, mirrored reference shape.

sections.title: Source Code - code/data_management/
sections.intro: Catalog generation, validation, and one-off fix scripts. Run from the workspace root.

files.description: Regenerates task_names.json, process_task_index.json, process_task_crossref.md, and file_inventory.md from authoritative sources. Does NOT touch process_details.json or task_details.json.

Validator for process_details.json. Runs JSON Schema (shape) plus Python cross-checks (header counts, ID uniqueness, category_id referential integrity, alias name uniqueness, task_count consistency). Requires `pip install jsonschema`.
Validator for task_details.json. Schema check plus uniqueness cross-checks and an opportunistic verification that hed_process_ids resolve to processes in process_details.json.
One-off script that applied 52 new aliases across 48 processes on 2026-04-26 (alias generation pass). Idempotent (skips aliases already present). Retained as a record of the change.
Applies KEEP/DROP marks from the variation audit to task_details.json.
Cleans parentheticals and spaced slashes from canonical names in task_details.json. One-time cleanup; safe to re-run on clean data.
Adds a url field to references derived from doi.
Backfills justification fields on task variations.
Decodes HTML entities (&, <, etc.) in process definitions.
Draft Phase-2 extraction script that parsed process_reference.md + process_categories.md into process_details.json. Superseded by direct JSON authoring.
Temporary regeneration helper. Disposable.
Test harness for regenerate_derived_files.py.

sections.title: Source Code - code/literature_search/
sections.intro: Phase 1 through Phase 9 literature-search pipeline. Library modules and runnable phase scripts. See code/literature_search/README.md for module-level overview.

files.description: Module-level overview of the literature-search code: design notes for pub_id, canonical_string, PDF filenames, cache layout.
Pure functions: build_canonical_string, build_pub_id, build_pdf_filename. No network, no I/O.
pytest tests for identity.py: 15 fixture rows times 3 functions = 45 expected passes.
cache_get_or_fetch: date-stamped, idempotent on-disk cache for API responses. Cache files live under outputs/cache/<source>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<hash>.json.
Field-of-study mapping (OpenAlex / Semantic Scholar categories to HED process categories).
Reference normalization helpers (DOI canonicalization, title comparison, etc.).
Query builders for Phase 3 retrieval (S2 sub-queries, OpenAlex/EuropePMC phrase filters).
Phase 3 ranking and selection: phrase-gate filter, scoring, candidate ranking.
Renders Phase 3 ranked candidates as markdown files in outputs/phase3/candidates/ for human review.
Rule definitions for triaging existing references (specialty journals, generic-too-broad patterns, malformed-entry detectors).
Phase 2 triage script: applies triage_rules.py to current references, writes a report to .status/.
Resolves the curated historical-landmark list against current references; flags missing landmarks.
Applies DROP marks from human review back into process_details.json / task_details.json. Refuses to drop references with role=historical without an explicit override.
Phase 1 end-to-end infrastructure validation: hits all 5 API clients for 7 test papers, verifies pub_id determinism, checks cache idempotence.

Phase 3 systematic search runner. Queries Semantic Scholar (per-alias sub-queries), OpenAlex, and EuropePMC; applies phrase-gate filter; ranks and writes candidate markdowns to outputs/phase3/candidates/.
One-off migration that unified the pre-2026-04-23 fundamental_references/recent_references split into a single references[] array with role tags. Already applied; retained as record.
DEPRECATED one-off: corrected 13 malformed references (Pavlov, Ebbinghaus, Sherrington, etc.) and dropped 2 entries. Already applied. Will not match against current schema.
DEPRECATED one-off: appended specialty journal entries to triage_rules.py. Already applied.

sections.title: Source Code - code/literature_search/clients/
sections.intro: API client modules for the five literature sources used in Phase 1 and Phase 3.

files.description: Package init.
OpenAlex Works API client.
CrossRef Works API client.
Europe PMC search API client (DOI or PMID lookup).
Semantic Scholar Graph API client. Uses S2_API_KEY from code/.apikeys to lift the rate limit from 1 rps to 100 rps.
Unpaywall API client (open-access PDF discovery).

sections.title: Source Code - code/citation_enrichment/
sections.intro: Legacy Phase A/B citation-enrichment scripts. Superseded by the literature_search workstream but retained as historical record.

files.description: Phase A: enriches process references against CrossRef.
Host-side wrapper for citation enrichment (run from Windows, network-accessible).
Host-side wrapper for task-citation enrichment.
Parses free-text citation_string entries into structured fields.
Tests for parse_citation_string.py.
Dry-run preview of task-reference parsing.
Resolves partial citations against CrossRef/OpenAlex.
Inspection tool: reports on enrichment quality (filled fields, unresolved entries).
Post-enrichment fixup pass.

sections.title: Plans, Task Instructions, and Thinking Documents
sections.intro: Reference docs that capture plans before they become work, and thinking summaries that capture rationale. Date-stamped. Not normally edited after the fact.

files.description: Top-level plan for the literature-search workstream (Phases 1 through 9).
Phase 1 (infrastructure) execution spec.
Phase 2 (triage existing refs) execution spec.
Phase 3 (systematic search) execution spec.
Thinking notes that led to the Phase 1 spec.
Thinking notes that led to the Phase 2 spec.
Thinking notes that led to the Phase 3 spec.
Curated list of historical landmark references (final).
Initial proposed landmark refs (pre-review).
First review of proposed landmark refs (Gemini + manual).
Second-pass review thinking.
Design rationale for the PDF naming convention.
Search-strategy decisions: which APIs, which fields, scoring.
Search-strategy thinking from an Opus session.
Sonnet-side revamp of the search task spec.
Plan for proposing aliases for the ~160 then-aliasless processes.
Cleanup plan for the catalog (consolidation, dedup).
Thinking notes for the legacy citation-enrichment workstream.
Spec for the legacy process-side citation enrichment.
Spec for the legacy task-side citation enrichment.
Spec for the 2026-04-19 consolidation of process_reference.md and process_categories.md into process_details.json.
Spec for the 2026-04-19 renaming pass that prefixed task IDs with hedtsk_.
Handoff doc for the process-criteria audit.
Variation-audit task definition.
Variation-audit execution spec.
Variation-audit thinking notes (Opus).
Variation-audit thinking notes (Sonnet).

Reviewed and approved alias proposals (52 new aliases across 48 processes, plus 2 hyphenation variants on Set shifting). Applied to process_details.json by code/data_management/add_aliases_2026-04-26.py.

sections.title: Outputs - outputs/phase3/candidates/
sections.intro: Per-item candidate markdown files from Phase 3 search; reviewed by humans (KEEP/DROP marks added in place). The current files are POC outputs from a pre-alias-update run; expect a regeneration once the new alias set is exercised.

files.description: POC candidate set for Model-based learning.
POC candidate set for Response inhibition.
POC candidate set for the Stroop Color-Word Task.

sections.title: Outputs - outputs/cache/
sections.intro: API response cache. Date-stamped; immutable once written. Tracked in git for reproducibility (the cache makes a re-run on the same day a no-op network-wise). Not enumerated file-by-file; layout below.

files.description: OpenAlex API response cache.
CrossRef API response cache.
Europe PMC API response cache.
Semantic Scholar API response cache.
Unpaywall API response cache.

sections.title: Git-Ignored Locations
sections.intro: Listed for orientation only. Not tracked in version control.

files.description: Session reports, decision notes, and historical thinking documents. Lives at the task-research/ parent level (not inside Claude-research/) and is gitignored. Filenames follow session_YYYY-MM-DD_<topic>.md and decision_YYYY-MM-DD_<topic>.md conventions.
API keys (S2_API_KEY, NCBI_API_KEY). One KEY=value per line. Gitignored. Never log contents.
Disposable temporary work files. Cleaned up between sessions.
